Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A sample event log from a manufacturing process CaseID A1 and B2 Task Group low level events into high level process steps Requirements Identify High Level Steps Justify Groupings Rationale Name High Level Activities Output Format Structured representation Goal Create a clear mapping from granular logs to meaningful workflow stages

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data Case A1 and Case B2 follows the exact same pattern as A1 just slightly different timestamps and specific IDs Observations The sequence is consistent across cases There is a clear chronological flow Resources change logically There is a distinct break between the Preheat step and the Welding step There is a Quality aspect embedded within the welding and at the end There is a Finishing aspect Coating and Drying

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting High Level Steps Segment 1 Retrieve Scan Place Align Preheat Theme Getting the raw material ready for the first operation Name Material Preparation and Setup Segment 2 Pick up tool Weld A Weld B Measure integrity Theme Joining the parts together Name Assembly and Welding Segment 3 Apply coating Dry coating Theme Protecting the surface Name Surface Finishing Segment 4 Visual check Theme Final approval Name Final Quality Assurance

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The measurement is a check during or immediately after welding to ensure the join is valid before moving on The coating cannot happen until the weld is verified So they are tightly coupled

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refinement Can Welding and Measure weld integrity be separated Option A Keep them separate Option B Group them Decision In manufacturing process mining immediate inline inspection is often part of the same logical step keeping them together is cleaner for process flow

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually looking at the resources Operator B does the welding Sensor 1 does the measure This suggests a transition Lets group Welding and Weld Integrity together as Welding and Verification

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Developing the Rationale Need to explain why these specific events go together Temporal proximity Events happen close together Logical dependency You cannot weld until it is preheated You cannot coat until it is welded Resource flow Operators and machines work in sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints Did I identify high level steps Yes Did I justify groupings Yes Did I name them Yes Is the format structured Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait looking closer Preheat is part of Preparation Lets try 4 distinct phases Material Preparation and Conditioning Welding and Structural Verification Surface Finishing Final Quality Assurance

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Measure Weld Integrity While it is a quality check it is specific to the welding If I group it with Welding it implies the welding step is not complete until measured This is accurate I will group them

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Plan Material Preparation and Conditioning Assembly and Welding Surface Protection and Curing Final Quality Inspection Wait is Visual Check distinct from Measure Weld Integrity Yes one is sensor based inline one is visual final
